item.title: How to Tell if a Programming Language Is Good or Bad
item.description: If you can write a bootloader using it, then it's a good programming language.
item.author: ronalds.vilcins@gmail.com (Ronalds Vilcins)

item.title: مشكلة المواقع المحظورة في مصر
item.description:
كذب الي قال إن الحظر مش بيُجدي وإن الناس هتجري على الممنوع المرغوب وتفتح VPN؛ ويكأن الـVPN هو أمر سلس، بسيط، سريع، إيفيشنت والذي منه، يقدر يعمله عم سعد وهو قاعد على المصطبه بعد ما فتح لينك الجزيرة الوثائقية بتاع عالم الحيوان ومشتغلش معاه.. حتى لو هو أعلم بتيكنولوجي الفيرشوال بريايفت نيتورك، مثلي، محدش هيروح يعمل مشوار الاتصال بالبروكسي علشان يتفرج على مشهد من عالم الحيوان أو يقرا سطرين لمعارضة فارغة.

وفي الواقع مشكلة الحظر في مصر تتخطى المشاكل السياسية، هي عملية غير منضبطة تمامًا راح فيها مواقع كتير بشكل غير مفهوم، إضاءات ظل محظور لفترة طويلة في مصر (وعلى ما أعتقد فُك الحظر) بدون سبب واضح (على الأغلب أحد العاملين بأجهزة الحظر شاف مقال معجبهوش) وفي إحدى المرات موقع bit.ly فضل محظور لفترة, تقريبًا عشان موقع معارض كان بيستخدمه كshortening service؛ فلما اتحظر الموقع اتحظر bit.ly معاه.

ما علينا من كل هذا، المشكلة الي كنت بعاني منها هي إني لو في يوم ما احتجت إني أفتح إضاءات أو القدس العربي وهما محظورين، كنت بروح مشاور كبير لحد ما الاقي vpn شغال يقدر يفتحهم ويconnect معايا. وكان فيه طريق تاني وهو إني استخدم tor browser عشان افتحهم بس كنت بكسل أستنى الtor connection لحد ما يتم, فلقيت طريقة لطيفة من حوالي سنتين تسهل البروسيس دي, وهي foxy proxy.

فوكسي بروكسي عندهم تول جميلة بتعمل VPN management, كل الي بيحصل إنك بتكتب wildcard أو regex إكسبرشن وبتديلهم البروكسي بتاعك (هيكون تور إن شاء الله). وبس. في أي مره هتدخل على URL بيmatch مع الإكسبرشن الي انت حطيته, هيستخدم تلقائيًا البروكسي بتاعك, وانت مش هتحس ان فيه اي حاجه حصلت. بس منين هنجيب البروكسي؟ صعب تلاقي static free proxy تستخدمه على طول ومتحتجش تغيره.. وكمان فري. فعشان كدا ممكن تستخدم Tor كproxy services.

Configuring Tor as Proxy Services on Unix #

هتحتاج تنزل tor و torsocks من الpackage manager بتاعك، بعدين هتفعل التور سيرفيس:

     $ sudo systemctl enable --now tor.service

اتأكد إنها اشتغلت:

     $ systemctl status tor.service

جرب تشوف الأي بي بتاعك حاليا من التيرمنال:

     $ wget -qO - https://api.ipify.org; echo

جرب دلوقتي تشوف نفس الأي بي لكن من خلال tor:

     $ torsocks wget -qO - https://api.ipify.org; echo

لازم يطلع أي بي مختلف.

كدا كفاية هنا لو هنستخدم البورت 9050, لو عايز تعمل كونترول بورت تقدر تغيره من /etc/tor/torrc:

      ControlPort 9051

Configure Tor as a Proxy Services on Windows #

هتدخل على موقع تور بروجكت وتنزل Expert Bundle exe, وتحطها في C:/Tor. بعدين أفتح PowerShell وأعمل:

C:\Tor\tor.exe --service install

Configure FoxyProxy To Use Tor #

أفتح الextension وأعمل add new proxy، حط في الHost: 127.0.0.1:9050 وأعمله socks 5؛ طبعًا لو غير ت البورت فهتكتب البورت المختلف بدل 9050، بعدين هتروح على الURL Patrerns وتحط الباترن الي عايز يطبق عليها الكلام الي قولناه. ممكن تنزل الملف ده https://raw.githubusercontent.com/salehmu/emacs-windows-config/main/local_storage_FoxyProxy%20Standard.json وتستخدمه بدل ما تقعد تعمل كونفيج بنفسك, انا حاطط فيه أغلب المواقع الي مكانتش بتفتح معايا مباشرة. بس لازم عشان تستخدمه تبص بصه على التول دي https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/storage-area-explorer/ocfjjjjhkpapocigimmppepjgfdecjkb
item.author: ronalds.vilcins@gmail.com (Ronalds Vilcins)

item.title: Colonial Memory (Arabic)
item.description:
أكثر الأشياء التي تستفزني في مآلات الذاكرة الاستعمارية، عند الشعوب المُستضعفة عمومًا، العرب خصوصًا اليوم، هي التصاق تأثيرات “صناعة العدو” بهم لليوم (enemy representation construction)؛ وهي نمط متكرر في معظم الحركات الاستعمارية التي عرفناها، ذكر الكثير من أمثلته سام كين في كتابه Faces of the Enemy: Reflections of the Hostile Imagination وهو كتاب ينظم أرشيفًا للعديد من تصورات البروجاندا الاستعماريةوالموجهة عسكريًا عن العدو، من أوروبا وحتى البروباجاندا الصهيونية.

النمط في هذه الصور كلها هو خلق صورة عن العدو، ليست فقط على الأغلب موجودة عنده، بل أيضًا هي موجودة عند الطرف الأول من الحرب (المُدعي) أكثر من غيره! وهذا حدث في الهند، أمريكا الشمالية، أفريقيا، وتقريبًا مع كل شعب مستعمر، وعلى عكس ما تصوره الانثروبيولوجيا الاستشراقية، كان المستعمر دائمًا أول مستفيد من حضارة المُستَعمر، وهذا الحال في جميع حالات شعوب القارات المذكورة، ففي أمريكا الشمالية مثلًا أُتهم الهنود بالبوهيمية وأكل لحوم البشر، بينما كانت الأخيره منتشرة ذلك العهد في أوروبا أشد انتشار، ولم يُعلم بشأن شعب أخر بهكذا آفة. وأتهمت الشعوب الشرقية بالقذارة في اعلانات الشركات الاستطياطنية الغربية، بينما لم يكتشف الغرب نفسه النظافة إلا متأخرًا. أما عن ارتباط هذا بالذاكرة العربية، فهو أمر متعلق قليلًا بأسطورة العنف الديني (i.e. الصراعات الدينية هي سبب معظم الحروب المفجعة والإرهاب)، وقد فندها وليام كافانو في كتابه the myth of religious violence, secular ideology and the roots of modern conflict؛ فادعاء أن المسلمين هم سبب سفك الدماء والإرهاب—وإن وُجد “إرهاب” تحت غطاء إسلامي—شاذ للغاية عند النظر لأقل صراع غربي حديث. فلتأخذ عملًا عسكريًا (ولن أسمه احتلالًا وإرهابًا) ‘عالميًا’ لإحدى القوى العظمى (هناك الكثير، وليكن العراق مثلًا) وأجمع ضحاياها المدنيين والأبرياء، وحجم الدمار الذي تسببت فيه، والآن قارنه بجميع العمليات التي قامت بإسم الاسلام، بل أضف إليها العمليات التي نصرت قضية ما كالعمليات الفدائية. لن تصل حتى لنسبة 5% من محصلة الخراب الغربي.
item.author: ronalds.vilcins@gmail.com (Ronalds Vilcins)

item.title: Death of Shireen and the Suitable Time to Push Things (Arabic)
item.description:
الثلاث “أزمات” الي ظهرت بإغتيال شيرين أبو عاقلة، وهي: 1. سوء الخطاب الوعظي وانعدام اخلاقيته/مهنيته. 2. انعدام التدقيق في صِحة هذا الخطاب من المؤمنين به انفسهم. 3. وهي نقطة حساسة، إلي أي مدى يُعد اغتيال شخص كشيرين أبو عاقلة أمر ‘critical’.

النقطة الأولى هي متعلقة ب"ماذا نقول ومتى نقوله"، أزمة الترحم على غير المسلم هي أزمة بتظهر في موت أي عَلم غير مسلم، ولكنها في منتهى العبث. فقول “الله يرحمه” هو مندمج اجتماعيًا كمعنى تواسي بين الناس، وأغلب الناس لا أعتقد إنهم بيستشعرونه نفسيًا (أو بصيغة الخطاب الوعظي: مش دعاء من القلب)، الأمر أشبه بالأصدقاء المسيحيين/النصارى الي بيستخدموا عبارات أندمجت في الثقافة الشعبية مثل “صلي على النبي” (لافتتاح الحديث) أو “لا إله إلا الله” لإظهار الامتعاض. التصاق هذه العبارات بالثقافة الشعبية هو أهم أسباب استمرارها وسط طبقة الغالبية المسحوقة التي لا تأبه كثيرًا بأمر دينها، بل مجرد بتحافظ عليه كمظهر اجتماعي. الطبقة دي كثيرًا ما بيحصل union join بينها وبين الخطاب الوعظي الي بيدعي لكثير من القيم الي اختلطت بالنسيج الاجتماعي، لكن منظرها ككل عبثي للغاية. فعلى سبيل المثال، أي حد من الأصوليين مدرك للغاية أهمية الصلاة، واحتمالية تكفير تارك الصلاة أو رَميه بالكفر، أو حتى قول إنه كُفرٌ غَير كُفر، كلها مآل عظيمة بالطبع، لكن هل ده يجعل للصلاة أهمية أكبر من الحفاظ على الحجاب كمظهر ديني عند أفريدج الطبقات المتوسطة بالمجتمعات العربية؟ على الإطلاق، نسبة الحجاب إلى نسبة المُحجبات المتلزمات بالصلاة قليلة. لكن الحجاب مظهر اجتماعي أكثر التصاقًا بالثقافة (ويمكن أسهل حفاظًا) من الصلاة. المراد قوله من كل ده، هو إن فعليًا لا أحد يأبه بوجود مراد فقهي لقولهم. في الواقع ده بيذكرني بأمر طريف حصلي زمان مع قريبة ليا سألتني عن حكم طلاء الأظافر (وكانت في نقاش مُحتدم مع إحدى نساء القرية الي كانت مُسحدثة الطلاء)، لما بحثتلها وجدت إنه حرام أو لا يجوز علشان بيمنع صحة الوضوء، وتَبرج. لكنها مكانتش محجبة ولا بتصلي.

بشكل أبسط: الترحم على الميت هي شكل ثقافي من أشكال المواساة. النقطة الثانية هي أهمية الخطاب الديني/الوعظي للمؤمنين به، أتمنى إني أكون أوضحت كفاية خطأ هذا الخطاب في الحديث حتى خصوصًا وقت وجود أزمة، وده أمر لا أخلاقي لأقصى حد ممكن ولا أُطيقة بصراحه، والي بيعمله نصيبه نزر من استحسان موضع الذكر واللباقة. لكن هل الخطاب ده صح؟ بالطبع صح. الي مش مؤمنين بالدين X مش عِباد صالحين في هذا الدين. وده أمر طبيعي وغير غريب على الإطلاق، الغريب جدًا هو وجود شخص مش مؤمن بالدين X وغاضب من هذه الحقيقة ومش متقبلها. محاولة مجابهة الخطاب الوعظي بتغليطه هنا هي محض عبث. لأنه صح.

وعشان نكون أكثر دقة، مسألة الترحم على شخص غير مسلم هي مسألة critical للغاية في الإسلام، وأسئلة مثل هل أبي طالب في الجنة، هل يمكن للنبي أن يشفع لأبويه ولعمه، هل جاز لإبراهيم الدعاء لأبيه، الخ.. هي أسئلة قديمة للغاية واجاباتها معروفة عند الأصوليين. لذلك بتعمل كل الضجة دي عندهم كل مرة.

المسألة الأخيرة، وهي حساسة كذلك، وهي عن مدى تأثير اغتيال شيرين كشخص. القتل الفردي مستمر في صفوف الفلسطينيين بغير حق طيلة الوقت، ليه موت شيرين كان أهم عند الميديا من جميع باقي الحالات الفردية الأخرى، الي بعضها شمل أطفالًا. الجزء الي بيُهم في الإجابة على السؤال، مدى تأثير ده تحديدًا على الأشخاص المرتبطين بهذه القضية، العرب تحديدًا. والسؤال ده حيزه هو نفس حيز انعدام اللباقة في مسألة الترحم، بالفعل الميديا مخطأه جدًا بتجاهل موت أي شخص والاهتمام بموت شخص ما بشكل أكثر، لكن الوضع ده يكاد يستحيل تغييره حاليًا، ونقده وإفناد الوقت فيه ملهوش أي عائد (مش صعب نبقى نكلم الناس عن ازاي الميديا شغالة)، الأصح استغلال الحادثة الاعلامية في زيادة التوعية، أما نقدها مضيعة للوقت وإهانة للكثير من النفوس. من ساعات ظهر خبر إن الكونجراس الأمريكي بيفكر في اقحام الFBI علشان تحقق في حادثة الاغتيال، بالطبع معروف إن ده نفس الكونجراس الي أعطى للاحتلال كل، أو مش لازم أكمل الجملة، أمريكا هي زارع الاحتلال وراعيه لليوم، والFBI هي أوسخ state police في الدنيا (بعد شبيحة أبو مازن)، انتظار إنه يحقق في مسأله كهذه غريب جدًا، لكن هل لو أدان ضابطين صهيونيين هيكون علينا الحديث عن ازاي أمريكا هي رأس الشر؟ بالطبع لأ، ده أمر يُناقش ثقافيًا بشكل مباشر، أما مثل النقاشات دي في حرب بتضف إليها حرب جديدة؛ حرب الاختلاف.
item.author: ronalds.vilcins@gmail.com (Ronalds Vilcins)

item.title: Did You Know That Saying World Wide Web Is Actually Quicker Than WWW?
item.description:
What the most unbearable thing to be right after running a javascript program? Yeah, that’s right, it’s writing a javascript program. I’ve made a terrible decision in the beginning of this year, that is, I started to rethink about Microsoft’s new technology aka .NET Core. I thought that it’s going to be the game changer ohh yeaah finally C# framework will run everywhere sooo fast tHaT’s cRaZyyy

Something I did not realize sadfully is: Microsoft sucks, whatever it makes. Just like any other corporate, it sucks. C# is a horrible slow ambiguit language, most of its frameworks are complicated as fuck. The deploying pattern of 99% of its packages are designed to be work with a shitty OS with no package manager component (like MS Windows lol). It is just bad. I’ve been writing C# for 5 months yet. And just yesterday when I wrote a small program in C++ to finish some job, then I just realized how slow C# was. It is too slow.

But I’m not to complain anymore. The reason of why I’ve been through this since the beginning wasn’t the .NET Core propaganda (ok not much at least), the but I wanted to create a very simple, very minimal, web blog. And I told myself, ASP.NET Froms has been out there for, I don’t know, maybe 20 years? Microsoft should be the most expert/old web framework publisher. Oh I can’t even describe how much it’s painful to understand the web binding in ASP.NET.

Let’s go further back, the reason behind creating a web blog is that I didn’t like how I manage this static HTML website, I wished if I can do more fancy things; mainly having a well-structured list for the books & cinema sections. Make it just more readable, nicer but not just a plain text. Everything else should render as it is. But since it is not that easy at all with .NET (at least so far with the problems I’m facing), and since I will have to write HTML and shit to design the end-user front, I gave up (or suspend) this project. But you know what I’m thinking of; I can do the fancy structure using Hugo. I think there are a functionality called hugo script or something that can replace text-tag with html tag in the rendering process (exporting to html), So I’m thinking of surfing a simple API that gives the movie/book data, and retrieve it in the section. I’m not sure how exactly I’m going to implement that but it is on my mind for this month. Wish me luck.
item.author: ronalds.vilcins@gmail.com (Ronalds Vilcins)

item.title: Game and Pornography and Instant Gratification
item.description:
I’m quite sure that “games” in the title might seem a quite subjective or unrelated word, at least doesn’t feel like a substitute or a par for the obscenity of watching porn, people don’t usually judge someone who is being identified as a ‘gamer’ a lot. For me, I believe that the costs of both are the same and each side is worse than the other one (maybe porn is worst because it fucks you up as well (and more important) the people around you more than games do). I’m going to explain this about video games because you usually have the regular objection “video games are just fun” [if you have an objection about porn then you have problems to solve bro].

Whenever I define video games as a type of instant gratification, social engineering and mind washing method, people start to consider me as the zealous regicide nerd who doesn’t want to waste his time, this happened 2 years ago when a major update was unleashed in the Linux kernel (I think it was supposed to make gaming easier), and I’m really not (at least this is not my self-image). I can build my argument against video games in 3 correlated points: 1. addiction. 2. (sigh) time. 3. the semi-delayed instant gratification, the third point is the most crucial to me.

I believe that gaming is a very fine art, in narrative game story you may find plots and values that you will not find in a lot of literature books. We all know a lot of games that have been turned into books and movies because of how much ingenuine it was. And this encounter the first 2 points. Games need a lot too much time than a movie, novel, even sometimes more than a TV show (which I also have concerns about) to rummage the values inside the game story. For me this is an adequate reason to quit.

The most critical cost of video games which makes it more dangerous for people (especially men) than porn, is how manipulative they are. Here I’m not talking about the insidious game design that makes you addict it (login twice a day, and you will get more PX!) the but how it generates fake-stimulated achievement, that is the leveling up. Pornography doesn’t do the same. It’s only a stimulated intimate and instant joy, its gratification mostly ends at the moment you’re getting to wash up, you might feel guilt about it, and you probably know already that it is a bad thing, this is not the case with video games.

The way that most of video games are designed is that they’re based on the concept of leveling up, which a very crucial concept in real-live. Conquer challenges and quests is the most joyous thing in the real life for a man who is doing something in his life. Just imagine how fun would it be if you live wild, gets your needs by your hands, the more you train and polish your self, the more you get gratifications (food, house, exp or whatever). This is what life have always been about. I think most religions can relate to that as well. This delayed-gratification is what makes the most of life, the noble values that you’re standing for, everything that you’re considering as magnanimity does mostly intersect with the values of delayed-gratification, everything that is malignant doesn’t (think about it).

We do need the delayed-gratification in our life to actually accomplish something. This is what ’loser’ mostly can’t do, sorry for using such a term but since this is informal blog it doesn’t matter, the loser can’t bear delayed-gratifications, guess what differs two people who live in shitty circumstances, but one passes and other suffers? the other chose to suffer.

Now the problem with video games is that it have this kind of delayed-gratification. We as humans have that predilection of achievements especially the delayed ones which makes the most of video games, you’re going wild, train, kill some people or do some missions, build good gear and challenge yourself more and more. This is literally what your brain would love to do in the real life but here is the thing: the leveling up is way more comfortable that it is in the real life, but even maybe more adventurous. Gradually your brain is going to love this kind of gratification, you don’t have to make a real-life achievements anymore to feel good because you’re a master in some online games. Maybe I’m being drastic about it, but ask yourself, how many times have you met an obsessed gamer who have minimum achievements in his/her life? None? I’m sorry for saying that but the most underdogs people I’ve ever seen are gamers.
item.author: ronalds.vilcins@gmail.com (Ronalds Vilcins)

item.title: The 'War' on Islam (Arabic)
item.description:
أتبدلت أفكاري مرات عديدة فيما يتعلق بمسائل حول الإسلاميين والإسلام السياسي خلال الفترات الأخيرة وفيما يتعلق بنظرتي للإسلاميين عمومًا، ما بين ضحايا للـfraternal deprivation، متزمتين، جهلة متعصبين، أو ملاك للحقيقة حتى. لكن في كل الحالات دي كنت بحترم شخص عبد الوهاب المسيري للغاية والـarguments الي بيقدمها رغم اني في مرات كنت بختلف معاه كثيرًا؛ he could make a well-built arguments1. بحب وصف أبو صديق أخواني أعرفه للمسيري: “أنأح راس في الفكر الإسلامي”.

أكثر شيء حبيته في المسيري هو فهمه للـsubsequence بتاع الغرب كويس جدًا (as an ex-marxist)، قادر يفهم كويس -أو يتصور- الأسباب الي بتدفع الغرب للشكل الي هو عليه اليوم وإيه العوامل الي بتحركه، الثقافة نفسها intrinsically، أو الرأسمالية والإمبريالية، البروباجاندا، الخ..

على عكس 99% من الخطاب الإسلامي الي شوفته؛ كل شيء بيحصل هدفه شيئان لا ثالث لهما: 1. حرب على الإسلام. 2. يُحِبُّونَ أَن تَشِيعَ الْفَاحِشَةُ. فقط لأن الأفعال الغربية غالبًا ما بتـintersect على أشياء بيحرمها الإسلام، مشكلتي مع هذا النوع من الخطاب هو إنه سهل جدًا التلاعب بيه لو أصبح له تأثير حتى (وهو احتمال مش وارد أوي لكنه مش ضعيف برضو). على سبيل المثال؛ نقاش متواتر بشوفه دايمًا حول نيتفلكس وليه المفروض تcancel it؛ الحجج بتبقى كالتالي: 1. أناركي بيكره أي corporate عمومًا وحابب الميديا كلها تgo with follow من غير فلوس. 2. البروباجاندا المَضمنه في العديد من الأعمال والdata tracking, mindwatching، ودي أسبابي شخصيًا. 3. فيها نساء كاسيات عاريات. في الواقع التلت أسباب are so valid، لكن الأخير is invalid when it comes to a political argument, هو دافع شخصي أكثر. المشكلة مع الدعوة الثالثة مش فقط جهلها بـconcerns لا تُعد، بل إنها لو أدت لتغيير يومًا ما, there will be always a workaround it، وده السؤال الي بسأله دايمًا؛ هل نيتفليكس لو عملت version بتـblur كل النساء الي في أفلامها، وكل المشاهد الفاضحة الي فيها هل ده يغير شيء؟ في الحجة الأولى لأ، في الحجة الثالثة؛ مش هيكون فيه حجة anymore. وده الأمر المختلف عند المسيري، المسيري كان عارف إن الprostitution، الثورة الجنسية، البروباجاندا، وثقافة الغرب، وغيرها، هي مجرد حاجات بتintersect أحيانًا مع مشاكل في الإسلام، لكن سبب الوقوف ضدها أهم من كده.

عشان كدا فيه غفلة دائمة على الخطاب الأوسع، وعلى الشركات الي بتبان محايدة وعادية فقط لأنها مش بتعمل حاجات صريحة غلط. حكم التعامل مع بنوك أو نتفلكس هو حرام، لأن كليهما بيعملوا حاجات محرمة بشكل صريح؛ ربا وعُري. لكن إيه حكم التعامل مع حاجه زي فيسبوك؟ جوجل؟ أمازون؟ كل الـcorporates الي غالبًا أصحابها لهم lobbying له تأثير أكبر مئات المرات على الشرق الأوسط كله، وهدفه واحد فقط؛ رؤس أموال تتضاعف (which mostly company with destruction, drones over our heads, corruption prepacked).

  1. عندي اعتقاد إن الأشخاص الي مروا بخطوات متعلقة بالبحث الأكاديمي في حياتهم بيبقى -غالبًا- عندهم قدرات جيدة على الاستنتاج/تبين الحق من الباطل. حتى لو دراسته مش متعلقة أوي بالـhumanities، يكفي فقط إنه يفهم الScientific method. ولعله رأي مُصيب أحيانًا ومخطئ أحيانًا أكثر. ↩︎
item.author: ronalds.vilcins@gmail.com (Ronalds Vilcins)

item.title: Childe Harold's Pilgrimage
item.description:
Whenever I glance or contemplate that painting from Elliott Daingerfield’s, my mind involuntary invokes these verselets by George Byron:

“..He who ascends to mountain-tops, shall find
⁠The loftiest peaks most wrapt in clouds and snow;
⁠He who surpasses or subdues mankind…
⁠Must look down on the hate of those below.
⁠Though high above the Sun of Glory glow,
⁠And far beneath the Earth and Ocean spread,
⁠Round him are icy rocks, and loudly blow
⁠Contending tempests on his naked head,
And thus reward the toils which to those summits led.”

Perhaps Daingerfield couldn’t show the same ‘proclamation of opprobrium’ for those who will never sense the toils nor the summits, as Byron did. However, I could discern every iota of pride and glory in the painting very well, just as in Byron’s words.

A crucial point to attaining summits is that, it is never demands being a part of the greatest creation or in some \(x\), the sensation is a part of the soul where you can find it. Thus, it can form from anything. وَلِلَّهِ الْعِزَّةُ وَلِرَسُولِهِ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَلَٰكِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ.

I love an old Arabic expressions of lack of pride and ignominy:

مَن يَهُن يَسهُلِ الهَوانُ عَلَيهِ ما لِجُرحٍ بِمَيِّتٍ إيلامُ

من كان هيِّنًا في نفسه؛ لا يستصعب ورود الهوان عليه؛ فهو كالميِّت الذي لا يتألَّم بالجراحة.
item.author: ronalds.vilcins@gmail.com (Ronalds Vilcins)

item.title: Back to the Origins
item.description:
There was some book or a disquisition, I don’t remember exactly what was it really about, but I can remember it was named “Back to the Origins”. This is the whole what I’ve been doing all the last period. Actually it’s likely that this will be what all I’ve done in my entire life. I’ve passed through a moral/legacy degeneracy from all what might be considered as something came from my testator (maybe in that case my pious father). That was a very inferior period of life, I can tell. Thankfully it didn’t last a lot. I don’t feel any regret about that period because I understood how much it was crucial to be the one who I am right now, I know exactly (or at least to some extent) how everything I’m standing for is constituted and why and its methodology. Something like the disbelievement of every std out there and writing all the std-implemented functions myself, so I know exactly what my program is doing. How ironic that I really used to do that. Anyway, I was just to say something about my sleep. I felt that the traditional sleep routine is not good enough, or possibly it was chosen thousands of years ago and being inherited, thus, it shouldn’t be suitable today. Hence today we can turn on lights and work at the night.

So I changed my sleep hours to be from 12:00 to 15:00 or sometimes 17:00 (yeah I can’t sleep a lot). It was something different. But sounds like it is, because of some kind of ‘invisible hand’ being mutated smoothly to match my old sleep hours form. Maybe the development of the traditional sleeping hours wasn’t the consequent of night’s darkness, maybe it’s the primitiveness of the human nature. The fitrah.

Or possibly it was because of the darkness. Why did I write all of this.

14/3 Update: I just figured the reference for “Back to the Origins”, it was CppCon’s Back to Basics.
item.author: ronalds.vilcins@gmail.com (Ronalds Vilcins)

item.title: Pervez Hoodbhoy's Islam and Science: How Not to Read History
item.description:
Introduction #

There is that Quranic expression from Bara’ah: He layeth his foundation [constructing] on an undermined sand-cliff, it is ready to crumble to pieces with him (Arabic: أَسَّسَ بُنْيَانَهُ عَلَىٰ شَفَا جُرُفٍ هَارٍ فَانْهَارَ بِهِ فِي نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ), a lot of arguments are based on such an “undermined sand cliff” which is really ready to crumble but the ‘argumenter’ doesn’t give a lot of attention of this, and he prolongs, decorates and fortifies his argument until the point it becomes a book: hundreds of page based on some inexact arguments. There are a lot of examples of this, American creationists exert a lot of efforts based on false beliefs, they even created a fully so called ‘scientific journal’ called biocomplexity which tries to prove that bio-objects are too complicated to be created by a blind watchmaker. This is led by the misunderstanding to every evolutionary fact, all the creationists claims however in how many pages it is descried, it is probably can be refuted in a few paragraphs which might sounds insanity to someone who watching this happens from afar.

Some chapters in Dr. Hoodbhoy’s, a Pakistani nuclear physicist, book are very applicable to this.

Methodology #

Hoodbhoy’s book consists of 12 chapters:

1. Islam and Science: Are They Compatible?
2. Science: Its Nature and Origins
3. The War Between Science and Medieval Christianity
4. The State of Science in Islamic Countries Today
5. Three Muslim Responses to Underdevelopment
      - The Restorationist
      - The Reconstructionist Line
      - The Pragmatist Line
6. Bucaille, Nasr and Sardar - Three Exponents of Islamic
7. Can There Be An Islamic Science?
8. The Rise of Muslim Science
9. Religious Orthodoxy Confronts Muslim Science
10. Five Great 'Heretics'
      - AI-Kindi (801-873)
      - AI-Razi (865-925)
      - Ibn Sina (980-1037)
      - Ibn Rushd (1126-1198)
      - Ibn-Khaldun (1332-1406)
11. Why Didn't the Scientific Revolution Happen in Islam?
12. Some Thoughts for the Future
      - Appendix: They Call It Islamic Science
      - The Scientific Miracles Conference
      - The Amazing Conclusions of Islamic Science
      - Is This Science?
      - Falsifiability: A Criterion for Science
      - What Islamic Science Actually Is
      - Its Political Roots
      - A Reponse S. Bashiruddin Mahmood
      - My Reply to Mr Mahmood
      - Comments from the Wall Street Journal

They can be categorized by 5 kinds:

  1. The nature of science and the history of the definition of science (Chapters 2, 3).

  2. The “state” of the “Islamic” science in the new world (Chapters 4, 12 and 5)

    Which in he ignores the political turmoil behind anything that happens in the Middle East (the overwhelming of what he calls the Islamic World).

  3. A historical background (Chapter 10).

    Or More precisely, a horrible historical background in which he tries to establish a constructing for the phantom war between “Science and Islam” by claiming that this war has been forever between the top Islamic scientist, the figures of the Islamic Renaissance: Ibn Rushd, Ibn Sina, Al-Kindi, AL Razi and Ibn-Khaldoun, he ignores every historical event in their lives.

  4. Mockery on many ignorants who claim to do science (and he designate it to Islamic Science) (Chapter 12)

    Hoodbohy makes a very good use of straw man arguments, for instance in chapter 5 “Three Muslims Responses to Underdevelopment” he chooses carefully the most lunatic responses to laugh on and refute.

  5. Sterile discussion on the nature of Islamic science (Chapters 1, 7 and 9)

This article probably will not be another response from the Islamic science proponents, but a critique of facts Hoodbhoy ignored especially the historical facts in chapter 10 Five Great Heretics. I will try to put on my views of faith and science in a conclusion.

The Forth Muslim Response to Underdevelopment #

The mushrooming of fundamentalist Islamic movements in the 1970s and 1980s is its most concrete manifestation. From nominally secular Egypt to the Wahabi Islamic Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, from the revolutionary Shi’ite state of Ayatollah Khomenei to the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, the trumpets call incessantly for Holy War!. Holy War against the secular, rationalist and universalist ideal. Holy War against capitalism, socialism, and communism alike. Holy War to establish one or other vision of an ideal Islamic state. Holy War to fight against the principle - first enunciated by the Arab (and Muslim) philosopher Ibn Rushd some 800 years ago - that human reason is the only instrument which should be allowed to guide human society. And Holy War against the foundations of modern, secular, scientific thought and method.1

This proem states the main problem with the book: the complete historical frame of all these events is being ignored. For instance the slump of the Arabic nationality and secular movements in the Arab world, which always had been on a feud with the Muslims Brotherhood (MB henceforth), one of the main causes of the re-rise of Islamic fundamentalism in the Arab world, and it’s also one of the most influential on Jamaat e-Islami2 which Hoodbhoy designates as partition of the ‘restorationism line’, who knows how it would go if Israel did not exist to kill the core of Arab secularism in Egypt (Gamal Abdelnasser3) so he could continue to put pressure on them, or even if there were no Saddat to fund Islamists in Egypt to defend communism and left-wing, if the U.S. didn’t fund all these pro-wahhabi movements in the 80s-90s, Saudi Arabia alike, we wouldn’t see a lot of figures of neo-Sahwa by the day, as well a lot of people that helped to establish the sick Islamic modern awareness.

There is a lot of consequential events that led to where we are today, all of it is not really related to Islam rather than the Middle East and its political unrest, actually we will observe that this is mostly the main reason [speaking of political issues] of the phantom war between science and Islam in the following paragraphs.

Dr. Hoodbhoy used in his book what I can name the most lunatic ‘responses’ to refute from Jammat e-Islam, which thinks that “All modernist ideologies are characterized by man worship. Man worship most often appears under the guise of science. Modernists are convinced that progress in scientific knowledge will eventually confer upon them the powers of Divinity.”4 as Maryam Jameelah, a spokesperson of it, puts. Also, its founder thinks that “geography, physics, chemistry, biology, zoology. Geology and economics are taught without reference to Allah and his Messenger and are hence a source of gumrahi (straying from the truth)”5.

So I’m going to skip the three restorationist, reconstructionist, pragmatist lines, because they don’t really represent a response to the underdevelopment.

Jamal al-Din al-Afghani and Hussine Fahmy #

However, Hoodbhoy began to establish the phantom war between science and Islam in this chapter, in The Prgamist line speaking of Shiekh Gamal El Dine Al Afghani

In 1870 Afghani was expelled from Istanbul under pressure from the clergy. His crime was the advocacy of a Darul-Funun, a new university devoted to the teaching of modern science.6

Hoodbhoy quotes this event from An Islamic Response to Imperialism Political and Religious Writings of Sayyid Jamal ad-Din al-Afghani by Nikki R. Keddie, which is not really a biography, however Keddie himself said about this event that “no text of the talk seems to exist”7 and he refuses to take Muhammad Abdo’s, who had been there, testimony because it is ‘possibly doctored’ he says [Note that Keddie wrote this in a similar context to Hoodbhoy’s. Possible bias]. Here is the story from a copy of Abdo’s testimony8:

And after sex months in AlMa’raef Majles, Afghani had been straightforward and righteous, and he sought to the popularization of Al Maraef, which would deduct a lot from Hussin Fahmy’s, Imam of Istanbul then, money, so he was mad on Afghani.

And in Ramadan 1287 hāʾ, Dar Al Funoon president asked Afghani to give a speech to talk about industries, Afghani refused because his Turkey wasn’t good enough, but Tahseen Afandy insisted, so Afghani wrote the speech and showed it to Sawfat Basha, the minister of Al Ma’araef then and a lot of other representatives of Istanbul [See all the names in the original text] and all of them liked it.

Afghani compared industry with some biological organs and faith, and in somehow Hussein Fahmy, the Imam who had in fuel with Afghani before, interpreted it as Afghani was saying that “Al Nubwa” (means Allah’s revelation) is a kind of industry so in somehow he says that the Prophet was just a cleaver literal and the result is he must be Kafer.

Fahmy went to the preachers in Istanbul, and as powerful cleric he threatened them to spear the publicity that Afghani spears disbelief and blasphemy.

[…]

I think it’s clear now why Fahmy was the only one to be mad of Afghani’s speech however the overwhelming of Istanbul representatives liked it, because he was mad on him since the popularization of Al Maraef, it was not a muslim response to Afghani because he talked about science otherwise he would have been expelled from all countries he had visited.

Underdevelopment in The New Arab World #

Lots of modern literature tried to re-explain the underdevelopment in the Arab world (e.g. Cammett, Melani. 2018) to a lot of reasons like the modern colonial heritage or even corruption. The question of underdevelopment might be discussed further in the future in this blog, however, I’d like to talk in brief about it.

Hoodbhoy believes, like me, that the overwhelming majority of people in the Islamic world are being satisfied by the belief of the pragmatist line: “pragmatist Muslims still form the majority. The electoral defeats of the fundamentalist parties in several Muslim countries strongly suggest that most Muslims will not accept fundamentalist versions of the Faith when there is an alternative”9. However, this put him in a contradiction: as the most people are not fundamentalists, and there are some countries without an underdevelopment issues (The U.A.E., Qater, etc…), why we can’t remark science in these countries?

We can give a lot of arbitrary social reasons, for example, the majority of people that live in a country like the U.A.E. might not be interested even in doing science, the U.A.E. people have been in tribes to the last century, they are new to things like schools and university, it’s very discursive that they won’t be interested in science like other peoples. These countries are just out from either long colonial eras or a long marginalization: even during the Abbasid and Ottoman Caliphate, these countries wasn’t an interest to any of the ruling powers.

This is applicable to any of what Abdul Rahman Munif calls “Cities of Salt”, for instance, try to guess when the first university in Saudi Arabia was established? It is not even more than 100 years ago, it was about in 1950, U.A.E. had its first university in 1976.

In the other side, the countries with more articulate history in science (e.g. Egypt, Syria, Lebanon and Iraq) are suffering from political upheavals in the daily basis Lebanon is going through an endless crisis, and it does not emerge from one war unless it enters another. Egypt and Iraq are going through two phases that are the worst in their history. 30% of people are under poverty threshold in the official statements in Egypt, such a people shouldn’t be every interested in doing science.

Five Great ‘Heretics’ #

In this chapter Hoodbhoy gives us examples of the holy insane war between Islamic fundamentalists and science, he gives five examples, five heretics: Al-Kindi AI-Kindi (801-873), AI-Razi (865-925), Ibn Sina (980-1037), Ibn Rushd (1126-1198) and Ibn-Khaldun (1332-1406). I have not studied Islamic philosophy before, but I’m pretty sure Hoodbhoy doesn’t know a lot of those philosophers’ history nor their history.

The main argument in this chapter here is that the following scientists had been accused of disbelief.

Al-Kindi (801-873) #

I’m glad that Hoodbhoy started with Al-Kindi, because his story is the most retable to my argument that most of Islamic philosophers and scientists who had been accused by disbelief, wasn’t accused of a religious view rather a political issue with the governor (Khalifa).

Hoodbhoy designates Al-Kindi to be a one of Mu’tazilite, which is inaccurate, there is no any evidence that Al-Kindi was one of them, in fact, there is very rare books about him in the current time, Islamic historian are not even sure about his previous religion before he converted to Islam as the Islamic historian Abu’l-Hasan Bayhaqi says:

And they weren’t sure of Al-Kindi, some people said he was a Jewish then he converted to Islam, and others said he was christian.10

You can find a lot of funny stories about Al-Kindi with Abu Tamam and other writers, but there is a story that you will find only in Hoodbhoy’s book:

In the court of AI-Mamun, AI-Kindi was a star who shone bright in the foremost cultural centre of the world. His academic pursuits maintained their vigour in the subsequent reign of the rationalist Caliph al-Mutassim, and then of al-Wathiq. But then came the ascendancy of the orthodox Sunni Caliph AI-Mutawwakil, and with it the end of a long period of liberalism. It was not hard for the ulema to convince the ruler that the philosopher had very dangerous beliefs. Mutawwakil soon ordered the confiscation of the scholar’s personal library, known to all Baghdad as al-Kindiyah. But that was not enough. The sixty-year-old Muslim philosopher also received fifty lashes before a large crowd which had assembled. Observers who recorded the event say the crowd roared approval with each stroke.

What a sad fake story, sadly I couldn’t examine Hoodbhoy’s reference here, he says in the footnotes that it is The Genius of Arab Civilization by J. R. Hayes, which is not available at all, however, after I searched in mostly all Islamic historic book that mentioned AL-Kindi, I can insure by 99% that this is an inaccurate story.

At least not the part of that the fundamentalist Mutawwakil that order to give the philosopher 50 lashes because he had been a very bad philosopher.

Nevertheless, the part about his issue with Al Mutawwakil and specially the classification of his library did happen, but not for the reasons that Hoodbhoy assumes. The Islamic historian Ibn Abi Usaybi’a tells us the full story in his book Lives of the Physicians [‘Eyoun Al Inbaa’ fi Tabiqt Al Atibaa’]11:

Al-Kindi had been on feud with Banū Mūsā, a family group of scientists and engineers who served the caliphs on the royal court alongside with Al-Kindi, however, Al-kindi was the most one of them to be closer to the Caliphate which Banū Mūsa didn’t like very much, so they told him that Al kinndy “criticizes you politically and spreads disbelief” then, Al-Mutwakkel ordered to classify all of Al-Kindi books and put it somewhere called “Al Maktaba Al Kindiah” where Banū Mūsā studied it.

The way that some friend of Al-Kindy manged to get back his library happened as the following: Al Mutawkel wanted to build a Dam, he ordered Banū Mūsā to take the responsibility of it, unfortunately they failed him and all of them were about to die (as they destroyed many resources and materials, they were sure that Al Mutawkeel was going to kill them all), so they rushed to Sanad ibn Ali, one of the best engineers ever in the Islamic world and Al-Kindi best friend then. Ali was aware of Banū Mūsā’s plan to bring Al-Kindi down, so he required them to bring Al-kendy’s library back again to him otherwise he wouldn’t help. And this is how Al-Kindy got his library back.

Thankfully some historians had written about how Al-Kindy dead, and it doesn’t have anything to do with the sad fiction story that he “couldn’t recover from the ordeal of the public flogging”. ‘Alī ibn Yūsuf al-Qifṭī says in his popular book “The biographies and the books of the great philosophers”:

And Abu ‘Yaqoub [Refers to al-Kindi] had a problem with drinking, and he had repented of it lately and started to drink Sharab Al Asal [means the honey drink, I’m not sure what is it], which clotted in his blood, so he dead12

This is all about Al-Kindi, as I said there was no evidence that he was one of Mu’tazilite, actually Hoodbhoy gives an example of his Mu’tazilitism by saying that he interpret a verse from Quran ‘rationally’:

To give an example of the allegorical reasoning that AI-Kindi believed in, consider verse L V5 of the Qur’an. In this passage, the believer is told that the sun, moon, stars, mountains, trees and beasts ‘bow themselves’ before God. For the unsophisticated, this invokes an image wherein all creation literally bends in prayer. But AI-Kindi gave an elaborate linguistic argument that the Arabic word for ‘bow’ should be understood as meaning ‘obey’. Thus, the naive picture of universal worship should instead be understood as the universal obedience to God’s will.13

I’m not sure where Hoodbhoy read this, as I said I couldn’t a digital format of his source, but anyway this is not a rational interpretation at all rather a regular one. Give a look at Al-Baghawi’s Taffsir, and hi is a one of the top fundamentalists in Islam:

The prostration was said that it was in the sense of obedience, for there is no inanimate object that isnot obedient to God, submissive, and has a glorifying one for him14

Averroes, Ibn Rushed (1126-1189) #

Hoodbhoy says:

Ibn Rushd, as Qazi of Seville and later Cordova, became the victim of political intrigues and a target for the orthodoxy. When the Caliph Abu Yaqub died in 1184, and was succeeded by his son Abu Yusuf, Ibn Rushd soon fell out of favour. A prohibition was issued against the study oflogic and science by order of the Caliph. Ibn Rushd was eventually banned from Cordova and was unceremoniously carted off to a small provincial town together with other students of philosophy. All his books, except for some strictly scientific ones, were ordered burnt. It was only towards the end of the 12th century that he was restored to favour, and returned to Marrakesh to die15.

I’m speechless in correcting this, I have no choice, so I’ll translate subchapter from Ibn Rušd biography16.

A lot of translated books tried to guess the reasons of Averroes’ catastrophe, and all of it is nothing but guesses attempt to discover the real accusation that Averroes was sued for. It was “Things in his books, it was said that it was written by his own hands” and one of these “things” is that he descried the giraffe in his book “The Animal” and said “I’ve seen it in the royal court of Barber kind” which frustrated Khalifa Abd al-Muʾmin al-Manṣūr of him, and this funny story continues and make up that Averroes tried to apologist for that, and he said to al-Manṣūr that he meant king of Birin17 not of Barber but the reader confused it with barbar.

And this is completely false story, as The Animal Book was written in Sevilla in 565 hāʾ, as Averroes said himself, it was when he was a judge for Abi Youssef Y’qoub, about 6 years before al-Manṣūr governess, and 15 years before the court.

[…]

However, the writer of Mu’jab18 that we made a use of before, suggested two reasons: one great and the other cryptic, and it is the main reason, he says “It was the great antagonism of al-Manṣūr because Averroes didn’t serve him in the best way that a Khalifa used to be served with” and he mentions the giraffe story which, as we mentioned above, is very inaccurate and has a lot of contradictions. And the ‘cryptic’ reason (the main one) is because Averroes said that Venus is a god, and we excluded this possibility because it isn’t relatable to him at all.

We have only two possibilities try to understand the reason of the catastrophe:

One of them is that it was a pretext, and the main reason —indirect reason— is Manṣūr’s aversion to Averroes because Averroes used to tell him that his behaviour is not inappropriate to a king, and what was said the Córdoba philosopher wasn’t complimenting him enough, instead he used to talk to him like “listen to me, my brother!”.

And the second one is what historians were unanimous on, that people of Córdoba didn’t like Averroes at all because he had been better than them, they envied and competed with him in the proximity to the Khalifa. […]

Al-Jabri after that continues to explain the reason that he think is the real one, and it is a book on politics, in which Ibn Rushd appears criticize the rule of al-Mansur. You can observe the following: There is no any exist of a war between orthodox fundamentalists and a rationalist Averroes but three possibilities:

  1. Averroes criticized al-Manṣūr policy in his book of politics.
  2. People of Córdoba made up an affliction between Ibn Rushd and al-Manṣūr by deliberately misunderstanding his words on Venus.
  3. The Caliph punished Ibn Rushd because he did not praise him well.

Ibn Khaldun (1332-1406) #

Hoodbhoy says:

It is a sad commentary on the state of Muslim scholarship that Ibn Khaldun remained a virtual nonentity until he was discovered by Orientalists. Now that he has their stamp of recognition, many scholars - excepting Arab racialists and the extreme orthodox - have entered into a competition to see whose encomiums are the loudest.19

It seems that Hoodboy couldn’t find anything about the infidelity of Ibn Khaldoun, as he was loved by most people in his ages, even by fundamentalists in our days, I don’t know who are the Arab racialists and the extreme orthodox that he talks about, however he gives us an example:

  • Taha Husain, the modernist Egyptian scholar, describes Ibn Khaldun as a man with an obnoxiously inflated ego and a dishonest rationalist who merely masqueraded as a Muslim.

So, Taha Husain The biggest enemy of Azhar and the fundamentalists in the 1990s, who criticized political Islam in such a way that Muslims themselves doubted his faith.. is one of the Arab racialists and the extreme orthodox. I’ve no comment on that.

Conclusion #

The Holy War #

You might notice that I didn’t give a response to Hoodbhoy’s claims about Avicenna and al-Razi, and that’s because: I couldn’t find any relabel biography for al-Razi, I didn’t find also any reference that talked about Hoodbhoy’s claim about his blindness, however I found that one of the greatest fundamentalists in Islam, Imam Zahbi, Praises Abu Bakr Al-Razi, which means he couldn’t be a heretic.

And the reason for skipping Avicenna (Ibn Sina) is that he is the one that could be named a ‘heretic’, I’m not the one to talk about his, but the main point is that the mean reason that made a lot of fundamentalists accuse him of infidelity is his issues with sufi metaphysics (Arabic: وِحْدَّة الوُجودِ) it was not related to his science at all, even Ibn Taymiyyah, the godfather of Sahwa, fundamentalists and even terrorists movements, absolved him of infidelity after he had read in ibn Khallikān’s biography about him that he “have repented of all of his late heresy”20, however Hoodbhoy prefers to believe that they just didn’t like that he was doing science and medicine, which is insanity.

Underdevelopment #

I do have a problem with Hoodbhoy’s presumed causes of underdevelopment in the Islamic world, as I said before, the Islamic Its presentation was and is still vulnerable to colonial and imperial movements and political upheavals, all of this affect the whole educational process. As I said before, people of Syria won’t probably think about doing science while Israel bombs Damascus.

  1. Hoodbhoy, P. (1991). Islam and Science: Religious Orthodoxy and the Battle for Rationality. Zed Books. ↩︎

  2. Liu, J. (2010, September 15). Muslim Brotherhood and Jama’at-i Islami. Pew Research Center’s Religion & Public Life Project. https://www.pewforum.org/2010/09/15/muslim-networks-and-movements-in-western-europe-muslim-brotherhood-and-jamaat-i-islami/ ↩︎

  3. Chomsky, N. (2004). On Religion and Politics, Noam Chomsky interviewed by Amina Chaudary. Islamica Magazine. https://chomsky.info/200704%5F%5F/: The attitude toward Islam is quite complex. The U.S. has always supported the most extreme fundamentalist Islamic movements and still does. The oldest and most valued ally of the U.S. in the Arab world is Saudi Arabia, which is also the most extremist fundamentalist state. By comparison, Iran looks like a free democratic society – but Saudi Arabia was doing its job. The enemy for most of this period has been secular nationalism. U.S.-Israeli relations, for example, really firmed up in 1967 when Israel performed a real service for the U.S. and Saudi Arabia. Namely, it smashed the main center of secular nationalism, (Gamal Abdul) Nasser’s Egypt, which was considered a threat and more or less at war with Saudi Arabia at the time. It was threatening to use the huge resources of the region for the benefit of the population of the countries of the region, and not to fill the pockets of some rich tyrant while vast profits flowed to Western corporations. ↩︎

  4. Hoodbhoy, P. (1991), ibid, pp. 52 ↩︎

  5. Ibid, pp. 53. ↩︎

  6. Hoodbhoy, P. (1991), ibid, pp. 53. ↩︎

  7. Keddie, N. R. (1983). An Islamic Response to Imperialism: Political and Religious Writings of Sayyid Jamal ad-Din “al-Afghani” (Volume 21) (Near Eastern Center, UCLA) (First ed.). University of California Press, pp. 17. ↩︎

  8. الأعمال الكاملة للإمام الشّيْخ محمَّد عَبْده: ترجمة جمال الدين الأفغاني. (ca. 1899). Quran Link. https://www.quran.link/books/89/101/ ↩︎

  9. Hoodbhoy, P. (1991), ibid, pp. 63. ↩︎

  10. Bayhaqi, A. ’.-H. (1154). Tarikh Hokmaa Al Islam [Bayhaqi’s history]. مطبة الترقي. https://shamela.ws/book/273/5 ↩︎

  11. Usaybi’Ah, I. A., van Gelder, J. G., & Cockrell, S. H. (2020). Anecdotes and Antidotes: A Medieval Arabic History of Physicians (Oxford World’s Classics) (Abridged ed.). Oxford University Press. http://islamport.com/w/tkh/Web/340/199.htm ↩︎

  12. Al-Qifṭī, ‘Alī ibn Yūsuf (2005). Shams al-Dīn, Ibrāhīm (ed.). Ikhbār al-‘Ulamā’ bi-akhbār al-ḥukamā’ (tr. The biographies and the books of the great philosophers) (in Arabic) (1st ed.). Lebanon: Dar al-kotob al-Ilmiyah. http://islamport.com/w/trj/Web/295/161.htm ↩︎

  13. Hoodbhoy, P. (1991), ibid, pp. 111. ↩︎

  14. Al-Baghawi, A. ū. M. ḥ. (1100). The Interpretation of al Hajj: 18. Tafisr Qura’n. https://quran4all.net/ar/tafsir/4/22/18 ↩︎

  15. Hoodbhoy, P. (1991), ibid, pp. 114. ↩︎

  16. This biography is from Al-Jabri, Muhammad Abed (1998). Ibn Rushd: Sīra wa-Fikr (ابن رشد: سيرة وفكر) [Ibn Rushd: life and thought]. Center for Arab Unity Studies. pp. 62-64 Al-Jabri is one of the contemporary philosophers and thinkers, and he devoted a part of his research to the study of Ibn Rushd ↩︎

  17. Birin (Arabic: بِرِين) means the two lands in Arabic, refering to the Land of Morocco and Andalusia. ↩︎

  18. Refering to Abdel Wahid al-Marrakushi, The history of the Almohades, preceded by a sketch of the history of Spain from the time of the conquest till the reign of Yúsof ibn-Téshúfin, and of the history of the Almoravides, ed. R.P.A. Dozy, 1968 (reprint of the second edition, Leyden 1881; first edition Leyden 1847) ↩︎

  19. Ibid, pp. 116. ↩︎
